import org.junit.jupiter.api.Disabled;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.Ignore;

// JV007 (skip): a disabled test must carry a non-empty reason. A bare marker or an
// empty / whitespace-only string value on @Disabled (JUnit 5) or @Ignore (JUnit 4)
// fires; a non-empty reason is clean, and a non-literal value expression is trusted.
// Matching is by simple name (no symbol solving) and there is no `// no-report:`
// escape - the fix is always to put the reason into the annotation value.
@Disabled // want "JV007"
class SkipWithoutReason {

    @Test
    @Disabled // want "JV007"
    void bareMarkerOnMethod() {}

    @Test
    @Disabled("") // want "JV007"
    void emptyReason() {}

    @Test
    @Disabled("   ") // want "JV007"
    void whitespaceReason() {}

    @Test
    @Disabled("flaky upstream, issue 42")
    void nonEmptyReasonIsClean() {}

    @Test
    @Ignore // want "JV007"
    void bareIgnore() {}

    @Test
    @Ignore("legacy, replaced by IT suite")
    void ignoreWithReasonIsClean() {}

    @Test
    @Disabled(value = "") // want "JV007"
    void normalFormEmpty() {}

    @Test
    @Disabled(value = "reason")
    void normalFormWithReasonIsClean() {}

    @Deprecated
    void unrelatedAnnotationIsClean() {}
}
